recalls.Description:
Nexgrill is recalling over 10.2 million metal wire bristle grill brushes that feature black plastic or wood handles and measure between 18 and 21 inches. This recall specifically affects model numbers 530-0024, 530-0024G, 530-0034, 530-0039, 530-0041, and 530-0042, all of which have the name 'Nexgrill' marked on them. The brushes were distributed between 2015 and 2026, with the exception of model 530-0042, which was sold until 2021.

The hazard arises from small metal wire bristles that may detach from the brushes, posing a serious ingestion risk that could lead to significant internal injuries requiring surgical intervention. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Nexgrill Recalls 10.2 Million Metal Wire Bristle Grill Brushes Due to Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Nexgrill at 800-942-1498 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://nexgrill.mktpoint.com/recall or www.nexgrill.com and navigate to 'Product Recalls' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nexgrill 19-Inch Grill Brush (Model Number 530-0024)
Recalled Nexgrill Grill Brush with Scraper (Model Number 530-0024G)
Recalled Nexgrill Long Handle Grill Brush (Model Number 530-0034)
Recalled Nexgrill Grill Brush and Scraper (Model Number 530-0039)
Recalled Nexgrill Grill Brush with Scrub Pad (Model Number 530-0041)
Recalled Nexgrill Wood Handle Grill Brush (Model Number 530-0042)

Injuries.Name: Nexgrill is aware of at least 68 reports and reviews where small wire bristles detached from the grill brushes, including five reports of consumers who swallowed metal bristles and sought medical treatment to remove the bristles from their digestive tract or throat.

Retailers.Name: Available at Home Depot stores and online at homedepot.com from 2015 to 2026, priced between $5 and $15.

Importers.Name: Nexgrill Industries, Inc. of Chino, California

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard from detached metal wire bristles

Remedies.Name: Stop using the grill brushes and contact Nexgrill for a gift card refund. Consumers will receive a registration code to upload a photo of their brush for the recall process.

recalls.metaDescription: Nexgrill recalls over 10 million grill brushes due to ingestion hazards. Learn how to get a refund and stay safe.

recalls.Description: Weber is recalling metal wire bristle grill brushes that feature plastic or wood handles and range from 12 to 21 inches in length. This recall affects specific models, including 6277, 6278, 6463, 6464, 6493, and 6494, which can be identified by their packaging. The brushes may pose a risk as small metal bristles can detach and potentially stick to food, leading to serious internal injuries requiring medical intervention.
recalls.Title: Weber Issues Recall for 3.2 Million Metal Wire Bristle Grill Brushes Due to Ingestion Risk

Injuries.Name: Weber is aware of at least 38 reports and reviews where small wire bristles detached from the grill brushes, including four reports of consumers who swallowed metal bristles and sought medical treatment to remove the bristles from their digestive tract or throat.

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's, Home Depot, Ace Hardware, Target, Amazon, and Weber.com from 2011 to 2026, priced between $10 and $17.

Importers.Name: Weber-Stephen Products LLC, of Palatine, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion from detached metal bristles

Remedies.Name: Replacement with a nylon bristle grill brush

recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using these grill brushes immediately and reach out to Weber for a replacement nylon bristle grill brush. Discard the recalled products as instructed.
recalls.metaDescription: Weber recalls over 3.2 million grill brushes due to ingestion hazards. Learn how to get a replacement.

Model numbers include various options from WL33006DMN to LL25013DMNSD, among others. The model number can be found on the unit's motor housing or impeller housing, depending on the type of vacuum.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of DR Power Leaf Vacuums Due to Laceration Risk; New Repair Kit Available
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact DR Power LLP at 877-221-4038 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@drpower.com. More details can be found online at https://www.drpower.com/LLV-FreeRepairKit or by visiting https://www.drpower.com/ and selecting "Product Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: Winston Products is recalling HydroTech 5/8-inch Expandable Burst-Proof Hoses manufactured on or before August 31, 2024. These hoses, available in lengths of 25, 50, 60, 75, 100, and 200 feet, come in various colors. The date code can be found on the end of the hose that connects to the spigot, visible after removing the black rubber washer. Hoses without markings or those with specific markings ending in -211 through -243 are included in this recall. Some packages may display the manufacture date in a month-year format, such as 0824 for August 2024, which also falls under this recall.
recalls.Title: Winston Products Recalls HydroTech 5/8-Inch Expandable Burst-Proof Hoses Due to Impact Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can contact Winston Products at 888-412-2396 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, via email at HydrotechVoluntaryRecall@hydrotechproducts.com, or visit https://hydrotechproducts.com/recall. More details can be found by clicking "IMPORTANT SAFETY RECALL INFORMATION" on their website.

recalls.Description: DR Power is recalling its 62-volt 5.0 Ah LiPRO rechargeable lithium-ion battery packs, which are used in various battery-powered products like lawnmowers, trimmers, and snow throwers. The recall includes standalone battery packs identified by model number 414240, as well as removable battery packs that come with specific DR Power outdoor equipment models including 524340, SB11022XEN, T4X3026XEN, T4X3062XENR, CE75021XEN0, and CE77021XEN0. The model number can be found on the battery pack's label, and the LiPRO and DR Power logos are displayed on the front and side panels of the pack. It is crucial not to dispose of these recalled batteries in regular trash or recycling bins due to their fire risk; instead, they must be handled as hazardous waste.
recalls.Title: DR Power Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery Packs Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DR Power at 800-454-8643 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@drpower.com, or visit https://drpower.com/recalls and https://www.drpower.com/ and select "Product Recalls" for further details.

recalls.Description:
Kawasaki Motors USA has announced a recall of specific engine models used in various lawn and garden equipment brands, including Bad Boy Mowers, BigDog, Bobcat, Cub Cadet, Hustler, John Deere, and Kubota. The affected engines, produced between 2021 and 2023, may overheat, leading to potential fire and burn hazards. A comprehensive list of affected engine models and serial numbers can be found on the Kawasaki website.

Consumers are urged to stop using any equipment with the recalled engines immediately. Owners should reach out to the respective brands or authorized dealers to arrange for a free repair. The recall affects a wide range of mower models and engine specifications, ensuring safety is prioritized for all users.
recalls.Title: Kawasaki Motors USA Issues Recall for Engines in Bad Boy Mowers, BigDog, Bobcat, Cub Cadet, Hustler, John Deere, and Kubota Equipment Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Kawasaki Motors Corp. USA at 866-836-4463 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.kawasakienginesusa.com/product-recall. Bad Boy Mowers owners can check their mower's status at https://badboycountry.com/kawasaki-recall. John Deere customers should call 800-537-8233 or visit www.deere.com for more details. For BigDog, Cub Cadet, and Hustler, reach the recall hotline at 888-848-6038 or visit their respective recall pages online. Kubota owners can call 888-458-2682 or check https://www.kubotausa.com/service-support/safety/safety-notices for more information.

recalls.Description: American Honda Motor has broadened its recall to include Honda HRN216 and HRX217K6 lawnmowers, along with GCV170/200 G5B engines used in pressure washers and as replacement engines for lawnmowers. These gas-powered walk-behind lawnmowers are identifiable by their red and black design and the "HONDA" logo on the clipping collection bag. Model and serial numbers can be found on a label on the upper rear of the mower deck. The pressure washer engines are mounted on various original equipment manufacturer frames, with the serial number engraved on a flat surface of the engine. Replacement engines are specifically designed for the Honda HRN216 and HRX217K6 models, with their serial numbers also engraved on the engine.
recalls.Title: Honda Expands Recall of Lawnmowers and Pressure Washer Engines Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach American Honda Motor at 888-888-3139 from 9 a.m. to 7:30 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or email at https://crmshonda.my.salesforce-sites.com/service/pew2c. More information is available online at https://powerequipment.honda.com/ by clicking on "Recalls and Updates" or by visiting https://cdn.powerequipment.honda.com/pe/pdf/Recalls/kp4.pdf.

recalls.Description: Oregon Tool has issued a recall for various log splitters and cylinder kits under the Oregon, PowerPro, Country Tuff, and Speeco brands. The recall affects models including the Country Tuff 25 Ton and 35 Ton Log Splitters, as well as several Oregon and PowerPro models. The brand names can be found on the side of the log splitters, while model numbers are located on the equipment itself. Consumers are urged to check their products against the recall list to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Oregon Tool Recalls Log Splitters and Cylinder Kits Due to Injury Risks

recalls.Description: Jugader is recalling its Zipline Kits due to a safety concern involving the stainless-steel cable, which may break and lead to falls. The kits include a 160-foot long, 4-mm thick cable, a blue trolley, and a plastic seat for hanging. Consumers should take this recall seriously to ensure safety during use.
recalls.Title: Jugader Zipline Kits Recalled Due to Fall Risk; Available Only on Amazon.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Jugader at 800-360-8078, email support@Jugader.com, or visit www.jugader.com/recall. You can also find more details by clicking on the 'Recalls' section on their website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Jugader Zipline Kit Cable and Trolley
Recalled Jugader Zipline Kit Seat

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of the cable breaking, resulting in nine injuries including lacerations and one broken clavicle.

recalls.Description: BJ's Wholesale Club has announced a recall of the Berkley Jensen Citronella 72-inch Tiki Torches due to potential burn hazards. These torches, measuring either five inches wide or six inches wide on a wrought iron stake, stand 72 inches tall and feature various torch tops including copper-plated, nickel-plated, black, or gray, along with a fiberglass wick. The risk arises from the possibility of the torch top breaking or detaching while lit, which could lead to burns.
recalls.Title: BJ's Wholesale Club Issues Recall for Berkley Jensen Citronella 72-inch Tiki Torches Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Customers are urged to stop using the recalled tiki torches immediately and reach out to BJ's Wholesale Club for a full refund. For assistance, contact BJ's Wholesale Club at 800-257-2582 during the hours of 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Saturday, and 12 p.m. to 6 p.m. ET Sunday. Alternatively, consumers can email through www.bjs.com/help/recall or visit www.bjs.com and select 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to torch top breaking or detaching while lit

recalls.Description: Char-Broil is recalling several models of its digital electric smokers due to a potential electric shock hazard. The affected models include the Digital Electric Smoker, Digital Electric Smoker with Window, Digital Electric Smoker in Black with Window, Digital Electric Smoker Medallion, Digital Electric Smoker Connected, and Digital Electric Smoker XL. These smokers are intended solely for outdoor cooking without charcoal and are available in black and stainless-steel finishes. The Char-Broil logo can be found on the front of the unit, while the product label, including model and serial numbers, is located on the back.
recalls.Title: Char-Broil Digital Electric Smokers Recalled Due to Electric Shock Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Char-Broil at 833-206-4647 from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Saturday, or via email at recall@charbroil.com. Additional information is available at www.charbroil.com under 'Product Recalls' or directly at www.charbroil.com/recalls.

recalls.Description: Husqvarna is recalling specific models of gas-powered grass trimmers due to a potential fire hazard. The affected models include the 330LK, 130C, and 130L, which feature an orange and gray design. The model name and 'Husqvarna' are displayed on the motor housing, while the SKU numbers can be found on the serial number tag located on the underside of the motor housing. A complete list of SKU numbers for the recalled models is provided for identification.
recalls.Title: Husqvarna Recalls Gas-Powered Grass Trimmers Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Husqvarna at 877-257-6921 from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at recalls@husqvarnagroup.com. More information is available online at www.husqvarna.com/us/recall or by visiting https://www.husqvarna.com/us/support/ and selecting 'Important Grass Trimmer Recall' at the bottom of the homepage.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to Traeger Flatrock Flat Top Grills that operate on propane. These grills are black, feature a closeable lid with a silver handle, and have the Traeger logo displayed in black. Measuring approximately 74 inches in length, 27 inches in width, and 36 inches in height, each grill weighs around 189 lbs. A silver label with SKU Number 1DFL42LLA can be found on the rear of the product.
recalls.Title: Traeger Flatrock Flat Top Propane Grills Recalled for Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the recalled Traeger Flatrock Flat Top Grills immediately and reach out to Traeger at 833-654-2407, available seven days a week from 6 a.m. to 10 p.m. MT. Additional information can be found at www.traeger.com/recall or by visiting www.traeger.com and selecting "Recall."

Products.Name: Traeger Flatrock Flat Top Propane Grill

Images.Caption: Recalled Traeger Flatrock Flat Top Grill
Silver Color label Located on the Rear With SKU Number 1DFL42LLA

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 57 reports of flame adjustment knobs being incorrectly labeled. No fires or injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Ace Hardware, The Home Depot, and Scheel's stores nationwide, as well as online at www.acehardware.com, www.homedepot.com, and www.traeger.com from February 2023 through October 2023, priced around $900.

Hazards.Name: Incorrect labeling of the burner control knob may cause the grill to remain unintentionally on, creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled grills and contact Traeger for inspection guidance. If the burner control knobs are found to be mislabeled, a free repair kit will be provided.

recalls.metaDescription: Traeger recalls Flatrock Flat Top Propane Grills due to a fire hazard from incorrectly labeled burner control knobs. Immediate action advised.
